channel.title: Luka Harambasic | Uses
channel.description: My private playground, publishing my thoughts and ideas. Showing of what I did and playing around with new technologies. In this feed you will stay up to date with my uses.

item.title: Apple MacBook Air
item.description: Specs: M1 (Processor), 16 GB (Memory), 256 GB (Storage)

item.title: ASUS ZenScreen MB16AC
item.description: Mobile display, powered over USB-C which gives me a dual display set up almost everywhere.

item.title: Bookmark Divider
item.description: A tool created by myself to use it as a bookmark divider between my favicon bookmarks.

item.title: Clipy
item.description: A clipboard manager for macOS. No more, no less.

item.title: Elgato Ring Light
item.description: Quite expansive, but the warmness (Kelvin) and brightness can be adjusted seamlessly (Mobile & Desktop).

item.title: Elgato Streamdeck
item.description: Programmable keyboard with displays which shows different keys depending on the context.

item.title: Enpass
item.description: I bought this password manager before they started the subscription model. Therefore, I can use it without any monthly fees.

item.title: Figma
item.description: Awesome vector tool, you can work live at files and share them easily.

item.title: GitHub Actions
item.description: As all my projects are on GitHub I'm just happy about such a simple solution for automation.

item.title: Google Workspace
item.description: In my case it is used privately, but it is also very easy to set up for small to large organizations.

item.title: google webfonts helper
item.description: Tool to help you host Google fonts without the CDN. Why should you care? GDPR, Performance, Control.

item.title: Logitech Streamcam
item.description: Supports default mounting options and produces a pretty good Full HD image.

item.title: Netlify
item.description: Connect Netlify to your repository to get a build when you push to main and also get previews in PRs.

item.title: Nuxt.js
item.description: Opinionated meta framework for Vue.js.

item.title: Playwright
item.description: A Node.js browser automation framework I use it to automate websites which won't give me API access or to generate images.

item.title: Rectangle
item.description: Move and snap windows in macOS via mouse and shortcuts.

item.title: Rode NT-USB Mini
item.description: Nice quality (as far as I can tell) and can be used without an arm.

item.title: Slack
item.description: If there is a new side project with multiple people I tend to directly create a workspace to streamline the communication.

item.title: Vercel
item.description: Same as Netlify, but I prefer Vercel for projects with serverless functions.

item.title: VS Code
item.description: As I'm not a big fan of TextEdit (macOS) I use VS Code as my primarily text editor. And nowadays also for Astro.js.

item.title: Vue.js
item.description: My favorite JS framework, as it lets you use what the web was made with: HTML and CSS! And Single File Components are just awesome.

item.title: vue-resume-component
item.description: My first npm package which powered the CV of a previous version of this page.

item.title: WebStorm
item.description: I like it as it just works and has all the features I need built-in.

item.title: WorkFlowy
item.description: My go-to knowledge app, you could also call it a note app. Basically it is an endless list.

item.title: AltTab
item.description: A better and customizable version of CMD + Tab on Mac.

item.title: Homebrew
item.description: Installing all the stuff the easy way, for nerds on Mac.

item.title: Raycast
item.description: Nicer version of Spotlight for Mac.

item.title: SvelteKit
item.description: Opinionated meta framework for Svelte (used for his website).

item.title: AirPod Pro 2
item.description: Had the first version, got the second one but sadly still have them with a Lighntning-Port. USB-C for the win!

item.title: Anker Prime Charger
item.description: As far as I know the smallest 100W charger (enough to charge a Macbook Pro) with 2x USB-Cand 2x USB-A.

item.title: Anker Nano Power Bank
item.description: Had a MagSafe Powerbank but as I'm now on Android I needed a replacement. Tech spec: 10.000mAh, 30W, integrated USB-C cable.

item.title: Vortrieb Modell 1.2
item.description: All black city bike for my daily commute. I love it. Just a front basket for my backpack is missing.

item.title: Camelbak Chute Mag (1.2 l)
item.description: I fell in love with big water bottles, my old one broke and I got this one as a present. Dropped it multiple times and nothing happened.

item.title: ChatGPT
item.description: I have the Plus version from work. I use it in different situations, dedicated post will follow.

item.title: Electric Tape (blue)
item.description: I'm getting around a bit, leacing with two room mates. I needed something to mark everything that is mine so that I won't forget it, but also other know that it's mine.

item.title: Fritzvold Minimal Wallet
item.description: I'm not a fan of these sliding wallets, but I also want something small that can hold a few coins and bills. Recycled paper artificial leather that is super durable.

item.title: Ghostty
item.description: Used Warp, but got to much AI, to much account, to much pushy in updates and it was slow. Giving Ghostty now a shot.

item.title: Google Keep
item.description: Use for more valatile notes, not knowledge that should be persisted, use it as "App" via Safari on my MacBook to have it in my dock.

item.title: iPad Air 13" (M2)
item.description: Always wanted an iPad with a large screen to also use it as a second screen when needed. And as I got to love the Apple silicons I also wanted at least a M1.

item.title: Loop Quiet 2 Kinetic
item.description: Got these earplugs recommended and have to say they fit perfectly, they dont fall out. I also love the little case so I can just throw them in my backback.

item.title: MacBook Pro 14" (M2 Pro)
item.description: My work laptop.

item.title: Notion
item.description: Used it for private stuff, heavily during my studies and now at work.

item.title: Perplexity AI
item.description: I use it for more complex search queries where I know I'll have to visit multiple sites to gather that information.

item.title: Pixel 9 Pro
item.description: Have been the last four years with the iPhone 12. Wanted to go back to the roots. With the most pinkish case. Post will follow.

item.title: Raspberry Pi 5 (8 GB)
item.description: Set up with Ansible and Docker, right now running Home Assistent with Zigbee. Next on the list: OpenVPN, AdGuard. Post will follow.

item.title: Standard Notes
item.description: My knowledge base, see it as how others use Obsidian. Out of the box sync on multiple devices and web access are important for me. The free version is enough and it is e2e encrypted.

item.title: Peak Design Tech Pouch
item.description: Bring a little bit more organization to your tech. I'm not using it at the moment as I heavily reduced what I bring with me. But might become more relevant again.

item.title: Todoist
item.description: My go-to todo list. It exists for that single purpose and doesn't try to be more. I have a pro subscription. I use it at work and for private tasks.

item.title: Wandrd PRVKE (21 l)
item.description: Water-resistant 21L backpack for biking to work and travel. Features attachable waist and accessory straps for versatility.

item.title: Withings ScanWatch 2
item.description: Love that people don't spot that it is a Smart Watch. Love the month-long battery time. But it's not working at the moment, need to fix it.
